items.content_html:
[PSA] RTFM and don’t try to charge 1.5V Li-Ion batteries with 1.2V NiMH chargers.

Also, having a patch of garden you’re willing to bury a burning battery in, is pretty useful.

items.title: Sidemount external filament mount
items.content_html:
Printed a side-mount external reel holder for the 3D printer. Saves me from awkwardly having to reach behind to mount an external reel, and also switching the PTFE feed tube.

This one works great with reels big and small, including the color samplers that I love!

As most folks with 3D printers know : easily half your prints are going to be some accessory for your hobby.

This model I found happens to include the Bambu logo, so I tried to do a color match.
items.content_text: Printed a side-mount external reel holder for the 3D printer. Saves me from awkwardly having to reach behind to mount an external reel, and also switching the PTFE feed tube. This one works great with reels big and small, including the color samplers that I love! As most folks with 3D printers know : easily half your prints are going to be some accessory for your hobby. This model I found happens to include the Bambu logo, so I tried to do a color match.

items.content_html:
Faced with the possibility of another night of washing dishes by hand, a $425 dishwasher installation quote, and visiting family, I was suddenly hit by a wave of inspiration and turned plumber for the evening.

Behold, I present you a dishwasher that I installed myself. And it hasn’t leaked (yet)!
items.content_text: Faced with the possibility of another night of washing dishes by hand, a _$425_ dishwasher installation quote, and visiting family, I was suddenly hit by a wave of inspiration and turned plumber for the evening. Behold, I present you a dishwasher that I installed myself. And it hasn’t leaked (yet)!

Also, turns out that the macro mode on camera on my iPhone makes a pretty good loupe!

items.content_html:
The concave side of the bathroom mirror broke, and I felt compelled to replace it rather risk a glass cut.

So I measured it out and designed a 6mm thick panel which I printed in PETG. Added a star to the design while I was at it, because, why not?
items.content_text: The concave side of the bathroom mirror broke, and I felt compelled to replace it rather risk a glass cut. So I measured it out and designed a 6mm thick panel which I printed in PETG. Added a star to the design while I was at it, because, why not?

items.content_html: I missed the days where computing was simpler, so I pulled some retro parts together with an M4 Mac Mini.
items.content_text: I missed the days where computing was simpler, so I pulled [some](https://support.apple.com/en-us/docs/displays/109732) [retro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_pointing_devices#Apple_Pro_Mouse_(M5769)) [parts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_keyboards#Apple_Keyboard_(109_and_78_keys)) together with an [M4 Mac Mini](https://support.apple.com/en-us/121555).
